page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r9i.3xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The instance is configured with 12 physical cores and 12 vCPUs, indicating a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio with no hyperthreading.

The instance provides 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through external network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps, and the instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes this instance class suitable for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and data analytics. However, the lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy operations. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent compute performance without resource contention from other tenants.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r9i.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor. Operating at 3.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 12 dedicated physical cores and 12 vCPUs with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The hardware configuration features 96.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-CPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage, requiring external network-attached storage solutions, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. With dedicated CPU allocation and a substantial memory footprint, the ecs.r9i.3xlarge is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as caching layers, in-memory databases, and data processing tasks that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
